info.topictitle: B3.4.7 Input sensors in digital systems
info.parenttitle: B3.4 Electronic systems application and selection (HL only)

markdown:
## Input Sensors in Digital Systems


<callout type="note">* Digital systems often follow an **input–process–output** model. 
* At the **input stage**, sensors are used to detect changes in the environment and feed this data into the system for further processing.</callout>

#### Common Sensors and Their Uses:


| Sensor Type | What It Detects | Example Use Case |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Accelerometer | Motion / tilt / vibration | Step counter in smartwatches |
| Ultrasonic | Distance / proximity | Parking sensors in cars |
| Photoresistor | Light levels | Auto-brightness in phone screens |
| Voltage sensor | Voltage / electrical levels | Soil moisture monitoring (moisture = voltage) |
| Hygrometer | Humidity & air temperature | Smart thermostats, weather stations |
| Pressure sensor | Atmospheric / barometric pressure | Altimeters in drones or aircraft |
| Microphone | Sound / audio waves | Voice commands in smart speakers |
| Infrared sensor | Heat / radiation | Motion detection in security systems |

<callout type="tip">When selecting a sensor, consider the **specific requirements **of the application, such as **accuracy**, **range**, and **environmental conditions**.</callout>

<callout type="example">* In a **smart irrigation system**, **moisture sensors **measure soil humidity. 
* If the soil is too dry, the system activates a pump to water the plants, optimizing water usage and ensuring plant health.

![Smart irrigation system (simple green energy)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/e739e70c84c4644dee09fac2a08c46105168f3da-2240x1260.png)</callout>

<callout type="example">#### Smartphones


* **Accelerometers** detect screen orientation and enable features like shake-to-wake.
* **Microphones** enable voice commands and noise cancellation.</callout>
